content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcar- PRACTTcBs CoMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
October 29,200I
Tom Wood, Assistant City Attorney
City of Costa Mesa
Post Office Box 1200
Costa Mesa, CA 92628-1200
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-216
Dear Mr. Wood
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Mayor Libby
Cowanregarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").t Pleas" note that the Commission does not provide advice relating to past
conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) ) Therefore, any conclusions contained herein
apply only to prospective actions.
QUESTION
May Mayor Cowan participate in decisions involving reuse of El Toro airport?
CONCLUSION
Provided she does not have an economic interest which could be materially and
financially affected by El Toro airport decisions, she will not have a conflict of interest
and may participate in such decisions.
FACTS
The City of Costa Mesa is considering issues related to the reuse of El Toro
airport which is a former Marine Corps air station that was closed by the U.S. Defense
Department for cost saving purposes. Libby Cowan is mayor of the City of Costa Mesa.
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commissionregulations appear at Title 2, sections
1 8 1 09 - 1 8997, of the Cnlifornia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-216
Page No. 2
Mayor Cowan is employed by the neighboring City of Irvine as a superintendent
in the Community Services Department. Irvine is vigorously opposing the proposal of
the County of Orange to reopen the closed airport as a public commercial airport. Flights
of commercial airplanes would cause disrupting noise for some Irvine residents, and
airport traffrc and air pollution would create other negative impacts on Irvine residents
and businesses. Irvine is supporting an alternative use of El Toro as a greatpark and has
applied to have the El Toro land annexed to the City of Irvine. Mayor Cowan's duties as
an Irvine employee do not include any responsibility concerning the future use of El
Toro.
The ultimate reuse of El Toro may have a significant impact on the residents of
the City of Costa Mesa, because if El Toro reopens as a public airport that will lessen the
need for expanding the county's existing major airport, John Wayne, which is located on
Costa Mesa's border. Expanding John Wayne Airport would tikely have negative
impacts upon citizens and businesses in Costa Mesa. The Costa Nri.ru City Council is
being pressured to take a public position on what is the proper use for El Toro and to use
city funds to support educating the public regarding the best reuse of El Toro.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials "perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
orthe financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 8100i.)
Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public offrcial from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest.
A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision within the
meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will
have a material financial effect on one or more of the public official's economic interests.
(Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) The Commission has adopted a standard analysis
for deciding whether an individual has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a given
governmental decision. (Regulation 18700(bX1)-(8) ) The steps of this analysis are
outlined below.
1. 
Is Mayor Cowan a public official?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to "public officials."
(Sections 87100 and 87103;Regulation 18700(b)(1).) "Public official" is defined as
"every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government
agency. . . ." (Section 82048 ) As the mayor of the City of Costa Mesa, Libby Cowan is a
"public official," and therefore, subject to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.


File No. A-01-216
Page No. 3
2, 
Will Mayor Cowan make or participate in making a governmental decision?
A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his or her position, votes on a matter, obligates or commits his or
her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractualigreement on behalf of
his or her agency. (Section 87100; Regulation 18702.1 ) A public official "participates
in making a governmental deCision" when, actingwithin the authority of his or hei
position and without significant substantive review, the official n.goiiut"r, advises or
makes recommendations to the decisionmaker regarding the governmentai decision.
(Section 87100; Regulation 18702.2.) A public official is attempting to use his or her
official position to influence a decision before his or her own uger"y if for the purpose
of influencing the decision, the official contacts or appears before any membe.,td...,
employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Section 87100; Regulition lB7O23 )
Mayor Cowan will "make a governmental decision" involving El Toro airport if
she, acting within her authority as mayor, votes on a decision involving the airport,
obligates or commits the city to any course of action regarding the airport, or enters into
any contractual agreement relating to the airport on behalf of the city. Additionally, she
will "participate in making" or "influence" a decision if she "rgug.r in any of the actions
detailed above.
3. 
What are Mayor Cowan,s economic interests?
Section 87103 provides that apublic official has a "financial interest" tL a
governmental decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official, a member of his or her immediate family," or on any of *r" official's economic
interests, described as follows:
A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
has a direct or indirect investment 2 of $2,000 or more (section g7103(a);
Regulation I 8703. 1(a)); or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section g7103(d);
Regulation 18703.t (b));
A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (section bzro:6;; Regulation
18703.2);
' An indirect investment or interest means any investment or interest owned by the spouse of an
official or by a member of the official's immediate family, by an agent on behalf of a pntti" oin"iul, or by
a business enlity or trust in r,vhich the official, the official's immediate family, or theiiagents own directl.l
indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent irterest or greater. (Section 87103.) 
-*Immediatl 
family,, is defined
at Section 82029 as an official's spouse and dependent children.


a
a
File No. A-01-216
Page No. 4
A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prioito the
decision (Section 871 03(c); Regulation I 8703.3);
A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if
the gifts aggregate to $320 or more within 12 months prior to the decision
(Section 87 1 03(e), Regulation 187 03.4);
A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal finances,
including those of his or her immediate family -- this is the "personal financial
effects" rule (Sectio n 87 L03 ; Regulation I 8703. 5).
Your question, only concerns whether Mayor Cowan has an economic interest in
the city of Irvine because it is a source of income to her. "Income,, is ,,a payment
received" and includes, in part, any salary, wage, or advance. (Section g20j0(a) )
However, income does not include:
"Salary3 and reimbursement for expenses or per diem
received from a state, local, or federal government agency
and reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem
received from a bona fide nonprofit entity exempt from
taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue
Code." (Section 82030(b)(Z)) (Footnote added.)
Consequently, Mayor Cowan does not have an economic interest in the City of
Irvine. Since a conflict of interest will arise only when it is reasonably foreseeable that a
decision will have a matertal financial effect on a public official's economic interest,
Mayor Cowan does not have a conflict of interest based on her employment with the City
of Irvine.
We note that you have provided information regarding only Mayor Cowan's
employer. For purposes of this letter, we assume that she has no other potential
economic interests relevant to decisions involving El Toro airport.a If Mayor Cowan
later concludes that she has an economic interest relevant to El Toro airport decisions,
she may wish to write in for further guidance.
3 "'Salary' from a state, local, or federal government agency means any and all payments made by
a governrnent agency to a public official, or accrued to the benefit ofa public official, as ionsideration for
the public official's services to the government agency. Such paymenti include wages, consultants,fees,
pension benefits, health and other insurance coverage, rights to compensated vacation and leave time, free
or discounted transportation, payment or indemnification of legal defense costs, and similar benefits.,,
(Regulation 18232(a).)
o Please bear in mind that this letter is based on the facts you have presented to us. The
Conrmission does not act as a finder of fact inproviding advice. (In re Oglisby (1g75) I FppC Ops. 71.)


File No. A-01-216
Page No. 5
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By j
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May Mayor Cowan participate in decisions involving reuse of El Toro airport?

CONCLUSION: Provided she does not have an economic interest which could be materially and
financially affected by El Toro airport decisions, she will not have a conflict of interest
and may participate in such decisions.
